name: fnllm

metadata.name: fnllm
metadata.summary: A function-based LLM protocol and wrapper.

documentation:
# fnllm

A generic LLM wrapper that provides a function protocol for LLM implementations. An OpenAI wrapper is provided.

# Getting Started

```sh
pip install fnllm
```

# Usage

Coming Soon

Deprecated and replaced by `tool_calls`.

The name and arguments of a function that should be called, as generated by the
model.

An optional name for the participant.

Provides the model information to differentiate between participants of the same
role.

class_variables.name: fnllm.config.json_strategy.JsonStrategy.LOOSE
documentation: Manual JSON parsing. Your prompt should request JSON, and the LLM will attempt to parse it, re-invoking the LLM if necessary to clean up the output.

class_variables.name: fnllm.config.json_strategy.JsonStrategy.VALID

class_variables.name: fnllm.config.json_strategy.JsonStrategy.STRUCTURED
documentation: The LLM is contractually obligated to return valid JSON that conforms to a schema.

functions.name: fnllm.caching.blob.validate_blob_container_name
documentation:
Check if the provided blob container name is valid based on Azure rules.

    - A blob container name must be between 3 and 63 characters in length.
    - Start with a letter or number
    - All letters used in blob container names must be lowercase.
    - Contain only letters, numbers, or the hyphen.
    - Consecutive hyphens are not permitted.
    - Cannot end with a hyphen.


container_name (str)
    The blob container name to be validated.

Returns
-------
    bool: True if valid, raises otherwise.

modules.name: fnllm.utils.sliding_window
documentation: Implementation of a sliding window based on time to calculate a moving average.

classes.name: fnllm.utils.sliding_window.SlidingWindowEntry
documentation: Represents an entry on the sliding window.

classes.name: fnllm.utils.sliding_window.SlidingWindow

methods.name: fnllm.utils.sliding_window.SlidingWindow.__init__
documentation:
Create a new sliding window with a given `time_window` in seconds.

For example, if `time_window = 120`, it will keep values for two minutes from when they are inserted.

methods.name: fnllm.utils.sliding_window.SlidingWindow.insert
documentation: Insert a new value into window, the timestamp will be the insertion time.

methods.name: fnllm.utils.sliding_window.SlidingWindow.sum
documentation: Get the sum of all values still in the window.

methods.name: fnllm.utils.sliding_window.SlidingWindow.avg
documentation:
Average per time window of all the values that have ever been inserted in the window (including the ones already outside it).

`result = [sum(all values) / (last_insertion_ts - first_insertion_ts)] * time_window`
Sliding window implementation. This tracks values for a given time `time_window`, always starting from the current time.
TODO: move this implementation to numpy for better performance
